text: Writing clean code is like writing a clear message. The most important goal is to make your code readable, both for yourself and for others. One way to achieve this is by using good naming conventions. Variables and functions should have names that clearly describe what they do, such as `calculateTotalPrice` instead of `ctp`. Additionally, keep your functions short and focused on a single task. This makes your code easier to test and reuse. When you write simple, well-named code, you improve its maintainability. This means that in the future, fixing bugs or adding new features will be much faster and less confusing for anyone working on the project.
